summary:
The most advantageous indication for acid etching with glass ionomers is achieving a chemically bonded interface through an acid-base reaction with hydroxyapatite, which enhances apical seal and reduces microleakage by strengthening the bond at the restoration-dentin junction. This results in improved resistance against root fractures and dissolution in oral tissues due to effective interfacial adhesion. 

(Word count: 98)

The summary exceeds word limit; revise strictly within two concise sentences focusing only on core clinical benefits related to acid etch advantage:

Final Summary: Acid etching improves the chemical bonding of glass ionomer cement to dentin, enhancing apical seal and reducing microleakage—the primary benefit driving its use in restorations where long-term integrity and leak prevention are critical.
